text:
Welcome!
The Accessibility Research Collective brings together scholars from multiple research groups as a way of sharing our processes, plans, and thinking as they evolve alongside contemporary social issues related to computing, disability, and design. We work in the fields of accessible computing, design, software engineering, and education and collaborate closely with community members through all phases of our work. Our efforts are informed by disability studies, critical theory, participatory design, action research, and more. We are committed to transforming research processes and practices to be more equitable and just. Our work is funded by the National Science Foundation, Toyota, Microsoft, and a UC Irvine Chancellor’s Inclusive Excellence Award.
© 2025
Department of Informatics
School of Information and Computer Science
University of California, Irvine

Irvine, CA 92697-3425
Research Opportunities
We are always seeking new undergraduate and graduate students to get involved in our research. Occasionally we have opportunities for paid undergraduate research positions. Please contact Stacy or Anne Marie if you are interested in joining our group. An excellent first step for undergraduates is to take a course in human-computer interaction. Prospective PhD students should apply through UCI's
Informatics PhD Program
.
